s1.title: Introduction
s1.p1: BookProof ("we", "our", or "us") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use our platform.
s1.p2: This policy applies to all users of the BookProof platform, including Authors, Readers, Affiliates, and Administrators.
s1.gdprText: For users in the European Union, we comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). You have specific rights regarding your personal data as outlined in Section 8.

items.label: Account Information
items.desc: Email, name, password, company name, phone number, country, preferred language

items.label: Profile Information
items.desc: Bio, profile photo, content preferences

items.label: Payment Information
items.desc: Credit card details (processed securely by Stripe), PayPal email for payouts

items.label: Campaign Data
items.desc: Book titles, descriptions, cover images, ebook/audiobook files, synopsis documents

items.label: Review Data
items.desc: Amazon profile links, review submissions, ratings

items.label: Communication
items.desc: Messages, support requests, feedback

items.label: Usage Data
items.desc: Pages visited, features used, time spent on platform

items.label: Device Information
items.desc: IP address, browser type, operating system, device identifiers

items.label: Cookies and Tracking
items.desc: Session cookies, authentication tokens, analytics data

items.label: Security Logs
items.desc: Login attempts, account actions, API requests

items.label: Payment Processors
items.desc: Transaction data from Stripe

items.label: Amazon
items.desc: Publicly available review data for verification

items.label: Analytics
items.desc: Usage statistics from Google Analytics (if enabled)

sub1.title: 3.1 Service Delivery
items: Create and manage your account
Process credit purchases and payments
Match authors with readers for campaigns
Deliver ebooks and audiobooks to readers
Process review submissions and verification
Handle payouts to readers and affiliates

sub2.title: 3.2 Communication
items: Send transactional emails (account verification, password resets, receipts)
Notify you of campaign updates, review deadlines, and important account changes
Respond to support requests and inquiries
Send marketing communications (only with your consent)

sub3.title: 3.3 Platform Improvement
items: Analyze usage patterns to improve user experience
Develop new features and services
Monitor platform performance and troubleshoot issues
Conduct research and analytics

sub4.title: 3.4 Security and Compliance
items: Prevent fraud, spam, and abuse
Enforce our Terms of Service
Comply with legal obligations
Protect the rights and safety of our users

s4.title: How We Share Your Information
s4.intro: We do not sell your personal information. We may share your data in the following circumstances:

items.label: Stripe
items.desc: Payment processing

items.label: Cloudflare R2
items.desc: File storage and delivery

items.label: Resend
items.desc: Email delivery

items.label: Upstash Redis
items.desc: Session management and caching

items.label: Neon PostgreSQL
items.desc: Database hosting

items.label: Sentry
items.desc: Error monitoring

items.label: Authors and Readers
items.desc: Limited information shared to facilitate campaigns (book details, content preferences)

items.label: Affiliates
items.desc: Commission data and conversion statistics

sub3.title: 4.3 Legal Requirements
sub3.body: We may disclose your information if required by law or in response to valid legal requests (subpoenas, court orders).

sub4.title: 4.4 Business Transfers
sub4.body: If BookProof is acquired or merged with another company, your information may be transferred to the new owner.

s5.title: Data Security
s5.intro: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your data:

items.label: Encryption in Transit
items.desc: All data transmitted via HTTPS/TLS 1.2+

items.label: Password Security
items.desc: Passwords hashed using bcrypt with salt

items.label: Session Management
items.desc: Secure JWT tokens with 24-hour expiration

items.label: CAPTCHA Protection
items.desc: Google reCAPTCHA v3 on sensitive forms

items.label: Account Lockout
items.desc: Automatic lockout after failed login attempts

items.label: Security Headers
items.desc: HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, and other protective headers

sub2.title: 5.2 Access Controls
items: Role-based access control (RBAC)
Multi-factor authentication for sensitive actions
Regular security audits and penetration testing
Employee access limited to necessary data only

sub3.title: 5.3 Data Breach Response
sub3.body: In the event of a data breach, we will notify affected users within 72 hours as required by GDPR and applicable laws.

s6.title: Cookies and Tracking Technologies
s6.intro: We use cookies and similar technologies to enhance your experience:

items.label: Authentication
items.desc: Keep you logged in (JWT tokens)

items.label: Session Management
items.desc: Maintain your session state

items.label: Security
items.desc: Prevent CSRF attacks and fraud

items.label: Preferences
items.desc: Remember your language and currency settings

items.label: Affiliate Tracking
items.desc: Track referrals for commission attribution (30-day cookie)

sub3.title: 6.3 Analytics Cookies (Optional)

items.label: Google Analytics
items.desc: Track usage statistics (requires consent)

items.label: Performance Monitoring
items.desc: Measure page load times and errors

sub3.note: You can manage cookie preferences in your browser settings or through our cookie consent banner.

s7.title: Data Retention
s7.intro: We retain your data for as long as necessary to provide our services:

items.label: Active Accounts
items.desc: Data retained while account is active

items.label: Inactive Accounts
items.desc: Deleted after 3 years of inactivity (or upon request)

items.label: Transaction Records
items.desc: Retained for 7 years for tax and accounting purposes

items.label: Security Logs
items.desc: Retained for 90 days

items.label: Marketing Data
items.desc: Deleted immediately upon consent withdrawal

s7.note: When you request account deletion, we provide a 30-day grace period during which you can cancel the request.

s8.title: Your Rights (GDPR)
s8.intro: If you are in the European Union, you have the following rights under GDPR:

rights.title: 8.1 Right to Access
rights.body: You can request a copy of all personal data we hold about you. Use the Export Data feature in your account settings to download your data package.

rights.title: 8.2 Right to Rectification
rights.body: You can update inaccurate or incomplete data through your account settings or by contacting support.

rights.title: 8.3 Right to Erasure ("Right to be Forgotten")
rights.body: You can request deletion of your account and all associated data. Use the Delete Account feature in your account settings. We provide a 30-day grace period before permanent deletion.

rights.title: 8.4 Right to Data Portability
rights.body: You can export your data in a machine-readable format (JSON) for transfer to another service.

rights.title: 8.5 Right to Object
rights.body: You can object to processing of your data for marketing purposes or based on legitimate interests.

rights.title: 8.6 Right to Restrict Processing
rights.body: You can request that we limit how we use your data in certain circumstances.

rights.title: 8.7 Right to Withdraw Consent
rights.body: You can withdraw consent for marketing communications or analytics tracking at any time through your account settings.

s8.contactNote: To exercise any of these rights, please visit your account settings or contact us at

s9.title: International Data Transfers
s9.intro: BookProof operates globally, and your data may be transferred to and processed in countries outside your region. We ensure adequate protection through:
items: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission
Adequate safeguards as required by GDPR
Compliance with Privacy Shield principles where applicable

s10.title: Children's Privacy
s10.body: BookProof is not intended for users under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we become aware that a child has provided us with personal data, we will delete it immediately.

s11.title: Changes to This Privacy Policy
s11.intro: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any material changes by:
items: Posting the new policy on this page with an updated "Last Updated" date
Sending an email to your registered email address
Displaying a prominent notice on our platform
s11.note: Your continued use of BookProof after the changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the revised policy.

s12.title: Contact Us
s12.intro: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your rights, please contact us:

s13.title: EU Supervisory Authority
s13.body: If you are in the European Union and believe we have not adequately addressed your concerns, you have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection supervisory authority.
